segment-text: Screenplay by
Nicholas Meyer & Denny Martin Flinn
Leonard Nimoy & Nicholas Meyer
We are determined to protect the secrecy of our materials, and of our project.
Your script is numbered and is for your use alone. In addition to your script number in each page, all scripts have been coded (by number) by a method
Reproduction of all or part of these materials will

Star Trek VI/Script It has come to my attention that unauthorized copies of the STAR TREK VI script have been made and distributed. Requests for copies of the script should only be made through my office (956-5797) Each script has been assigned both a number and an internal code, which allows us to trace the script even if

the red number written on each page is somehow removed. A wide distribution of the script weakens the impact that STAR TREK can have at the box office. Please return your script to my office if it is not essential to the making of this film. Please understand that this is

to help us deliver a fresh movie to our dedicated audience. Please help us maintain the secrecy of our project. Thank you for your help and cooperation. Credits OVER serene space when SUDDENLY... an enormous EXPLOSION, sending debris FLYING at WARP velocities...
INT. BRIDGE, USS EXCELSIOR

The polyglot CREW is busy. Seated in the CAPTAIN'S CHAIR, a familiar face: SULU. He has his own command and is drinking a CUP of tea. The SCIENCE OFFICER stands next to him, conferring, handing him a datacount. Sulu muses over the contents.
According to this we've completed our exploration of the entire sector. Fifty-four planets - and their gaseous atmospheric anomalies. Our
sensing and analytic equipment
Then it's time we were heading home. Three years is...
The ship starts to TREMBLE.
ANGLE ON SULU'S TEA CUP
shaking in its saucer... Sulu and the CREW look at each other as the SHAKING increases. The TEACUP SMASHES. I have an energy wave from 240
an ENORMOUS GASEOUS WAVE, a subspace shock front.
Meteors and asteroids SMASHING PAST. Burning chunks fly by at WARP speeds. EXCELSIOR reels.
INT. USS EXCELSIOR BRIDGE
The ship rolls, throwing everyone off balance.
The crew jumps to. Computers light up. Information flies across screens and monitors. The ship is BATTERED like a fighter's punching bag with shocks and jolts and tremors.
INT. CREW'S QUARTERS, GALLEY CORRIDORS, ETC.
Sleeping members are thrown from their berths. The CLAXON alarm sounds pervasively... What the -- Look out -stations -- etc.
INT. EXCELSIOR BRIDGE
Captain, she's not answering her
Starboard thrusters. Turn her into the wave!
INT. EXCELSIOR ENGINE ROOM
Chaos. Valves hiss PURPLE. Crew running, knocked about. Engine room. What's going on up -10
INT. EXCELSIOR BRIDGE
Quarter impulse power! EXCELSIOR has been knocked about and everyone on the
bridge is holding on or sliding around. She settles and one by one her CREW resume their stations.
Damage report.
Seems to be in one piece, Captain. We're checking all systems...
shows Excelsior getting bumped about by the final fragments drifting past, bouncing off the shields.

(to Science Officer) Don't tell me that was any meteor shower. (over his console) Negative. The subspace shockwave originated at bearing three-twothree, mark seven-five, the location is... (turns to Sulu) Praxis. A Klingon moon. Barren of indigenous life forms but SULU Essential as a resource. Praxis is their key energy production facility. (thinks) Send to Klingon High Command: "This is Excelsior, a Federation Starship traveling through Beta Quadrant. We have monitored a large explosion in your sector. Do you require assistance?" Aye, sir.
Mr. Valtane, any more data? I have confirmed the location, sir, but...
What is it? ... I cannot confirm the existence of Praxis.
Sulu reacts, goes over to the science console and looks at the screen. Nothing there.
Praxis? What's left of it. Captain, I'm getting a message from Klingon High Command.
Onscreen.
a snowy picture of a WILD-EYED KLINGON OFFICER.
suffered He looks around, the GROUND is shaking under him; as Sulu watches, the pictures collapse, then comes on again, with a different face.

This is Brigadier Kerla, speaking for the High Command. There has been an incident on Praxis. However everything is under control. We have no need for assistance. Obey treaty stipulations and remain outside the Neutral Zone. Transmission ends now.
The screen goes black. Sulu looks incredulous.
An INCIDENT? Do we report this, sir?
Are you kidding? (swivels to the communications officer) Send to Starfleet Command...
EXT. SPACE
Excelsior warps off into the galaxy. Kirk walks into shot.
What are we doing here?
INT. STARFLEET BRIEFING ROOM - DAY
The room fills with BEMEDALED BRASS, Kirk's CREW among them - the only starship complement in the room. They can't help looking at each other, questioning. They are in uniforms.
Search me. Anybody know how come we made the A list?
My shore leave was just cancelled. (reacting) Isn't this just for top brass?
Where is Mr. Sulu?
Captain Sulu... on assignment... anyone seen Spock?
A door opens and STARFLEET ADMIRAL DONALD, austere and impressive, enters and gavels the meeting to order.
This briefing is classified. There will be no recording devices. Ladies and Gentlemen, the C-in-C.
All rise as the STARFLEET COMMANDER IN CHIEF enters with a briefcase and pulls materials from it.
As you were. I'll make this as simple as I possibly can. The Klingon empire has roughly 50 years of life left to it.
They look at each other in disbelief.
(continuing) For full details, I am turning this briefing over to our Special Federation Envoy.
He makes a sign and another door opens. Spock walks to the podium. Kirk and company are astounded.
Good morning.

(continuing) Two months ago a Federation Starship monitored an explosion on the Klingon moon Praxis. We believe it was caused by over mining and insufficient safety precautions. The moon's decimation means an almost eighty percent loss of available energy and a deadly pollution of their ozone. Due to their large military expenditures, the Klingon economy is in a very weakened condition. They do not have sufficient resources to
manufacture air shelters in large
The dismantling of our space stations and star bases along the Neutral Zone, an end to seventy years of unremitting hostility - a hostility the Klingons can no longer afford.
Audible mutterings. PAN the crew. Is the war over?

(continuing) If the Klingons sue for peace, we could accumulate savings in defense expenditures, leaving the Federation economy free to grapple with urgent social problems... Bill, are we talking about mothballing the Starfleet?
I'm sure our exploration and science programs would not be affected, but the facts speak for themselves, Captain.

I must protest. To offer the Klingons a safe haven within Federation space is suicide. Klingons would become the alien trash of the galaxy. And if we dismantle the fleet, we'd be defenseless before a bellicose people with a foothold on our territory. The opportunity which presents itself here is to embargo trading, force them to run through their own resources faster, and bring them to their knees. Then we'll be in a far better position to dictate terms. A vocal minority of the briefing supports this, but not
all. Kirk simply stares at Spock.
Starfleet is under civilian control, Admiral. The decision is a political not a military one and it's been made.
Captain Kirk?
(eyes Spock briefly) Sir, I'm no diplomat, but the Klingons have never been trustworthy. I'm forced to agree with Admiral Donald. This is (he gropes for tact) - a terrible, a terrifying idea...
Your son was killed by a Klingon, wasn't he, Captain?
Yes, sir.
I'm sorry. But Commander Spock has persuaded the Federation that this situation is too promising to
It is imperative that we act NOW to support the Gorkon initiative, lest more conservative elements persuade his empire that it would be better to attempt a military solution and die fighting.
Captain Kirk, you are to be our first olive branch.
Me?
We have volunteered to rendezvous with the Klingon ship that's bringing Chancellor Gorkon here, and escort him safely through Federation space.
Kirk is too startled to speak.
As it happens, Kirk, the Chancellor specifically requested you and your officers.
Why, in God's name?
There are some Klingons who feel the same about the peace treaty as yourself and Admiral Donald. They'll think twice about attacking the Enterprise under your command.
I have personally vouched for you in this matter, Captain.
You have personally CinC You will accord Chancellor Gorkon full diplomatic Courtesy, Captain
(straining) Surely a full Ambassador would be better equipped to -
If there's no further business I wish you and your crew Godspeed. Thank you all.
He charges out as ALL RISE. Everyone files out.
I don't know whether to congratulate you or not, Jim.
He moves off before Kirk can reply. The room is empty... except for Spock, still at the podium and Kirk sitting alone in the auditorium. He is furious, betrayed. Spock knows it.
WE volunteered?
There's an old Vulcan proverb: only Nixon could go to China.
(incensed) How could you vouch for me? That's... (words fail him) ... arrogant presumption SPOCK I was asked by my father to open
I know your father's the Vulcan Ambassador for heaven's sake, but you know how I feel about this: they're animals.
Jim, there is an historic opportunity here KIRK DON'T TRUST THEM. DON'T BELIEVE THEM SPOCK They're dying.
LET THEM DIE.
Spock looks at him in silent reproach. Kirk hears himself and doesn't like it.
(and what's more) Has it occurred to you that this crew is due for retirement in three
months? WE'VE DONE OUR BIT FOR KING AND COUNTRY. Spock is patient. They stare at each other. ON Kirk's face.
(finally) You should have trusted me.
INT. THE BRIDGE, USS ENTERPRISE
The elevator doors open. An attractive VULCAN LIEUTENANT leaps out of the command chair, whips to attention.
Well, I've heard some whoppers in my time... (overlaps) They're mothballing the fleet?
(overlaps) We will regret this... Captain on the bridge.
The CREW stands to.
As you were. Lieutenant...? Saavik, sir. We were told you'd need a helmsman (to Spock) ... so I volunteered.
She salutes. He smiles, returns the salute.
Lieutenant, I am pleased to see you. (to Kirk) The Lieutenant is the first Vulcan to be graduated at the top of her class as the Academy.
Congratulations, Lieutenant. That must make you very proud...
I don't believe so, sir. BONES She's a Vulcan all right.
(down to business) All right, let's get this over with... departure stations... (pushes the comm) Scotty?
They assume their familiar stations.
Aye, sir?
You find the engine room? Right where I left it, sir.
Standby. Get me the dock master, Uhura.
Control tower, reading, Sir.
Control, this is Enterprise requesting permission to depart.
This is control. Enterprise, you are granted permission to depart... 30 seconds for port gates...
Clear all moorings...
Awaiting port gates from this mark. Mooring lines cleared...
(overlaps) Thank you. Lieutenant, one quarter impulse power...
(she turns to face him) Captain, may I remind you that regulations specify thrusters only
while in space dock?
The CREW - old and young - watch this exchange keenly. Spock is poker faced
(next to Chekov) Uh, Jim...
You heard the order, Lieutenant.
Saavik swivels away, rolling her eyes so Kirk can't see and hits the panel The ship begins to rattle slightly, then
EXT. SPACE DOCK
the Enterprise SCREAMS out of the dock and exits through the just-opening bay doors into outer space.
INT. BRIDGE, USS ENTERPRISE - NIGHT
Kirk sits comfortably with a smile on his face.
(continuing) Lieutenant... Saavik faces him.
(continuing) ... I don't care if I'm senile. If I sit in this chair and give the word you jump. Spock watches. Aye, sir. Plot a course for Kronos, Lieutenant. (startled) Kronos, sir?
I'm still in the chair, Lieutenant. Aye, sir.
Other reactions. Kirk has taken command.
EXT. SPACE
Enterprise cruises purposefully through the void.
INT. KIRK'S CABIN
Kirk is unpacking his bag as he dictates, hanging up his uniforms, his shaving tackle going next to the sink, etc.

(continuing) I have never trusted Klingons and never will. I have never been able to forgive them for the murder of my boy. Spock says this could be an historic moment and I'd like to believe him, but how on earth can history get past people like me?
He sets up a photo of DAVID next to his berth. A delicate COUGH. Kirk realizes he's not alone. Swivels to face LIEUTENANT SAAVIK standing in the bulkhead doorway.
Come on, Saavik, you COULD knock LT. SAAVIK We're almost at the rendezvous - I thought you'd want to know...
Right He gets up and puts on his jacket. She watches.
Permission to speak freely, sir?
He doesn't stop her.
(continuing) I gather you are not enthusiastic about the assignment... I don't think many on board are.
You piloted well out of spacedock,
(almost smiles) I always wanted to try that.
(passing her) Only don't try putting words in my mouth.
And leaves her with that.
INT. CORRIDOR OUTSIDE SPOCK'S QUARTERS
Saavik approaches; this time she's smart enough to KNOCK. SPOCK'S VOICE
INT. SPOCK'S QUARTERS
In contrast with his Spartan personality, the room is a voluptuous incense-laden lair, filled with curtains and smoke. Spock lights a votive candle; surveys her. He is out of uniform.
You have done well, Saavik. As your sponsor at the Academy I have followed your career with... satisfaction. And as a Vulcan.
(intense) Sir, I speak to you as a kindred intellect. Do you not recognize that a turning point has been reached in the affairs of the Federation?
I am not certain such speculations are included among your duties, Lieutenant. VOICE FILTERED Klingon battlecruiser off the port bow. All hands on deck. Repeat...
She's startled.
(off her look) In time you may come to view socalled turning points with... skepticism.
He starts to get into uniform.
(continuing) You must have faith.
That the universe will unfold as it should.
INT. BRIDGE, USS ENTERPRISE
The cruise is proceeding evenly as Kirk arrives out of one turbo-lift. Spock and Lt. Saavik emerge from another.
Captain on the bridge.
Captain, shall we raise our shields?
Spock looks at Kirk: trust me. Kirk scowls at him and stares at the screen. They all follow his gaze.
THE MAIN DISPLAY
(finally) Never been this close.
The Chancellor is undoubtedly awaiting our signal.
Boy is this hard for Kirk - finally:
Uhura, hailing frequencies. Right standard rudder, bring us alongside...
Right standard rudder, Z plus five degrees... Channel open, Captain.
This is the Starship Enterprise, Captain James Kirk commanding.
GORKON comes up on the main visual display.
This is KRONOS One. I am Chancellor Gorkon.
Chancellor. We've been ordered to escort you through Federation space to your meeting place on Earth.
Thank you, Captain.
(does his best) Uh, would you and your party care to dine this evening aboard Enterprise with my officers as guests of the United Federation of Planets?
A few of the crew look around at Kirk.
We'd be delighted to accept your gracious invitation.
(nuts) We'll make arrangements to have you beamed aboard at 1930 hours.
I shall look forward to it.
He disappears. Kirk turns to Spock.
I hope you're happy. Captain.
He turns.
Kirk smiles, relieved.
Chekov leans over to Uhura.
Guess who is coming to dinner.
Both ships cruise parallel in space.
INT. ENTERPRISE TRANSPORTER ROOM
Scotty stands by the console. Kirk, Spock and Bones stand next to him. They are spiffed up.
The beams reflect on the glass shield as half dozen Klingons, including one WOMAN start materializing. (NB Separate BEAM-INS for Gorkon, Azetbur, Chang). Gorkon descends first. He is tall, splendidly
surroundings. An ELECTRONIC BOSUN'S WHISTLE pipes
(continuing) Chancellor, may I present Commander Spock, whom I believe you know, Dr. Leonard McCoy, chief medical officer, Montgomery Scott, chief engineer...
(to Spock) Commander, face to face at last.. you have my thanks..
Spock bows.
(continuing) Gentlemen, this is my daughter Azetbur... my military advisor Brigadier Kerla... and this is General Chang my chief of staff.
Chang wears a PATCH over one eye. He advances on Kirk with a grim smile, lone eyeball to eyeball.
I've always wanted to meet you, Captain.
(smiles) I'm not sure how to take that.
Sincere admiration, Kirk CHANG (soft) As one warrior to another...
(ducks this) Uh, right this way, gentlemen. I thought you might enjoy a brief tour...
The party leaves. As they move past TWO CREWMEN...
They turn to find themselves facing an expressionless
You men have work?
Yes, sir.
Then snap to it.
INT. R DECK CORRIDOR
leaving the Enterprise Science Labs...
Your research laboratory is most impressive...
Starfleet's been charting and cataloging planetary atmospheres. All vessels are equipped with
chemical analytic sensors...
This cannot be easy for you, Captain... (off the look) I would feel awkward if I had to give you a tour of OUR vessel...
The man's courtesy makes Kirk feel guilty...
Would you care to go topside?
Very much.
(pulling Kirk aside) Captain, you're not going to show them the bridge??
(clenched teeth) Full diplomatic courtesy, Mr.
The party passes Uhura and a YOUNGER CREWMAN.
Would you want your daughter to marry one?
INT. OFFICERS' MESS, ENTERPRISE

A WINDOW IRISES OPEN REVEALING the stars gliding by. A HAND POURS DEEP BLUE ROMULAN ALE into Gorkon's crystal goblet. Dinner well underway. Principles from both parties are at table. STEWARDS keep food and drink in motion.
I give you a toast: the undiscovered country (off their looks) the future. All drink ROMULAN ALE, echoing "the undiscovered
country." The KLINGONS eat with their fingers, scorning utensils.
HAMLET, act three, scene one.
You have never experienced Shakespeare until you have read him in the original Klingon.
Chang turns to Kirk with a malicious twinkle.
"To be or not to be, that is the question" which preoccupies our people, Captain Kirk. We need BREATHING room...
I beg your pardon?
Captain Kirk, I thought Romulan ale was illegal.
It takes Kirk a moment to regain his composure.
One of the advantages of being a thousand light years from Federation headquarters.
Silence.
To you, Chancellor Gorkon: one of the architects of our future. All drink. As Romulan ale flows throughout the scene,
tongues get looser. Gorkon WATCHES.
Perhaps we are looking at something of that future here.
Commander Spock, mindful of all your work behind the scenes, and despite the cordiality at this mess, I do not sense an acceptance of our people throughout your ship.
They're naturally wary, ma'am. We've been at war a long time.
How do both sides overcome ingrained prejudice?
Perhaps with a few small steps at a time. Like this one. BONES And perhaps with a large step or two. Like a peace treaty.
Captain Kirk, are you willing to give up Starfleet?
Kirk stares.
(looks at Kirk) I believe the Captain feels that Starfleet's mission has always been one of peace KIRK (glares) Far be it for me to dispute my first officer. Starfleet has
Come now, Captain, this dinner is off the record: in space all warriors are cold warriors.
We have never tried to -KERLA You hypocritically presume that your democratic system gives you a moral prerogative to force other cultures to conform to your politics.
That's not true...!
No?
Uhura turns to the Klingon next to her - all tact.
General, are YOU fond of
He looks at her, his hands and mouth full of food. Ugh.
We do NOT impose democracy on others. We do believe that every planet has a sovereign claim to human rights.
(spits) "Human rights." Even the name is racist. The Federation is basically a "homo sapiens" only club...
SPOCK reacts to this. Chang is amused.
Present company excepted, to be sure...
Well, I suppose we're not perfect SCOTTY (rising) Don't let them put words in your mouth - ! I haven't served 30 years in the engine room of a starship to be accused of gunboat diplomacy!
In any case, we know where this is leading: the annihilation of our culture. Klingons will replace those on the lowest rung of the Federation employment ladder, taking menial jobs and performing
them for lower pay...
That's economics, not racism UHURA But you have to admit it adds up to the same thing.
Don't be naive, Commander -!
Who you calling naive -?
Kirk stares sullenly down the table throughout this.
We're explorers not diplomats!
Starfleet's killed an awful lot of natural phenomena in the name of "exploration"...
We follow orders...
Since when has THAT been an excuse? Diplomacy must resolve these SCOTTY Right - leave it to the politicians to muck it up and leave us defenseless...!
A COUGH interrupts the fight. All eyes on Gorkon. The Klingons conceal their amusement.
(finally) Well. I see we have a long way to go.
Reactions from Kirk and his officers.
INT. TRANSPORTER ROOM, ENTERPRISE
Both parties are gathered, saying goodbyes.
Thank you, Captain Kirk. The evening has been most... edifying.
(wooden) We must do this again soon.
Gorkon stares at him, smiles slightly.
You don't trust me. Kirk can't look at him.
(continuing) I don't blame you. If there IS to be a Brave New World, we old people will have the hardest time living in it. Commander Spock.
Chancellor. A pleasure to meet you, Ma'am.
Captain, Commander...
General Chang, a pleasure...
(grins) "Parting is such sweet sorrow, shall we say goodnight till it be morrow?"
Kirk is ready to slug him but turns his fist into a SALUTE. The Klingons step on the transporter platform. Chang speaks into his communicator, nods to Kirk.
Energize.
They disappear. The Bosun's whistle.
Did you see the way they ate? Terrible table manners.
I don't believe our own conduct will distinguish us in the annals of diplomacy...
I'm going to sleep it off. Let me know if there's some other way we can screw up tonight. He starts out; others follow...
INT. KIRK'S QUARTERS
He comes in slightly drunk and pushes the button on his log. Lies on his bunk and speaks. The SHIP'S BELLS give 0100.

Captain's log. Stardate 8679.15. The Enterprise hosted Chancellor Gorkon and company for dinner last night. Our manners were not exactly Emily Post. Note to the galley: Romulan ale no longer to be served at diplomatic functions.
He leans back, sighs, thinking KIRK
(filtered) Captain Kirk, you are wanted on the bridge. Captain Kirk...
Kirk reacts, tries to shake himself awake.
INT. ENTERPRISE BRIDGE
Kirk enters. Glances at the main visual display: the battlecruiser still travels silently alongside.
Captain.
(rubs his eyes) What is it? SPOCK
I am uncertain.
Spock, I'm really tired...
We are reading an enormous amount of neutron radiation.
(tries to focus) Where?
Curiously it appears to emanate from us.
From Enterprise?
Spock remains silent. Kirk is disturbed, paces, watching the displays.
Saavik, you know anything about a neutron energy surge? Sir?
Mr. Chekov, anything unusual?
Just the size of my head KIRK I know what you mean...
A photon TORPEDO suddenly streaks from the bottom of the screen and hits the hull of the President's
What the SPOCK We've fired on the Chancellor's
Torpedo room--?
Uhura, monitor! Direct hit UHURA Confirmed, Captain!
A second photon shoots out from the bottom of the display screen toward the President's ship
(frantic) Who's doing that? Saavik hits panel controls, trying to find out...
INT. GORKON'S STATEROOM, KLINGON FLAGSHIP
Gorkon, several advisors and soldiers were seated but the impact has thrown them about. They speak SUBTITLED The entire FLAGSHIP is drenched in AMBER emergency LIGHT.
Another blast. They start floating helplessly in the air! Everything mobile RISES...WEAPONS FLOAT OUT OF REACH... Gravity generator!
INT. TRANSPORTER ROOM, FLAGSHIP

TWO SETS OF HEAVY GRAVITY BOOTS as they materialize. The legs inside them wear STARFLEET UNIFORMS and carry PHASERS at their sides. They SHOOT the Klingon Transporter OFFICER. They STOMP HEAVILY through the ALARMS and chaos, firing at the helpless, floating CREW... CRIES of wounded... WEAPONS FLOAT out of their holsters just out of reach in the AMBER LIGHT...
INT. FLAGSHIP CORRIDOR
where Klingon crew are FLOATING helplessly. The GRAVITY BOOTS shoot anything in their way, WALK ON WALLS, etc. as they hurry along to
INT. GORKON'S STATEROOM, FLAGSHIP

where they BLAST a GUARD at the door, SEVERING HIS ARM and enter. The KLINGONS have weapons but are floating and unable to aim. They are quickly shot as the two HIT MEN make their way toward Gorkon. BLOOD NOW FLOATS about as well as debris in the crackling emergency lighting. A good deal of HISSING Chancellor Gorkon is shot. The Hit Men turn and exit quickly. Linger on a scene of WEIGHTLESS carnage and assassination.
INT. FLAGSHIP CORRIDOR - NIGHT
The Hit Men hurry back the way they came in their awkward magnetic boots. Bodies and blood floating around them
INT. TRANSPORTER ROOM, FLAGSHIP
The HEAVY BOOTS step on the platform and de-materialize.
INT. BRIDGE, USS ENTERPRISE
A snowy picture on the visual display, then Chang comes on (still in AMBER LIGHT)...
(He's screaming in Klingon, reverting to his native language under stress.) He says we've fired on them in a blatant act of war.
We HAVEN'T fired SPOCK According to the data bank, we HAVE - twice... Captain, they're coming about!
ONSCREEN
the battlecruiser/flagship now heading for Enterprise. They're preparing to fire.
Shields up, Captain --?
Kirk hasn't moved. He's staring at the display, thinking.
Uhura, signal our surrender. Captain -KIRK WE SURRENDER.
This is Enterprise. We surrender.
Captain, if they fire at us with our shields down -KIRK Torpedo bay! DID we fire those torpedoes?
INT. ENTERPRISE TORPEDO BAY
Scotty at the console.
CU DATA BANKS: they reconfirm - two photon torpedoes
Stand down your weapons. Captain, if -57
Stand DOWN, Mr. Scott. All stop. That's an order. Aye, sir.
Bones hurries in, carrying his stuff.
I wish I knew. Uhura? It's pretty chaotic over there. There's been some weapons fire
and a lot of shouting...
I'm going aboard. Spock, you have the conn.
He heads for the transporter room. Spock blocks him.
(blocks Kirk) I am responsible for involving you in this. I will go.
(bitter) I'M going. You are going to be responsible for getting me out of this. Meantime we're not going to be the instigators of a full-scale war on the eve of universal peace.
Perhaps you're right.
And Spock uncharacteristically pats him on the back.
(continuing) Good luck, Captain.
I'm going too. They may need a doctor.
Uhura, tell them we're coming. And tell them we're unarmed.
They hurry out.
INT. GORKON'S STATEROOM, FLAGSHIP

Chang and his ASSISTANTS are trying to sort things out without gravity. Chang shouts efficient orders in Klingon as he finds and tries to help Gorkon... Chang keeps himself upright by grabbing a handhold, but the stricken are still floating... A few more orders from Chang, then the lights flicker, then all the bodies and BLOOD slam to the ground as the gravitational source is restored. Lighting is "NORMAL."
INT. FLAGSHIP TRANSPORTER ROOM
Kirk and Bones materialize. They are quickly surrounded by ARMED SOLDIERS. Kirk puts his hands out to indicate they are not carrying weapons. They are searched as Kerla enters.
Have you lost your mind?
I give you my word I don't understand what has happened.
We're here to help...
(hesitates; furious) Follow me.
He leads them along the
INT. FLAGSHIP CORIDOR
same path - a scene of destruction - which the hit men took, right into
INT. GORKON'S STATEROOM, FLAGSHIP
where dead and wounded are being separated. Kerla hurries over to Gorkon, askew on a rug, blood running everywhere. Azetbur is holding him, now bloody herself (she wasn't in the room during the
He goes to him immediately. Chang rises from next to Gorkon.
My God, what happened here?
You feign ignorance?
WHAT HAPPENED?
You crippled our gravitational field with a direst torpedo hit, and two Starfleet crewmen beamed aboard in magnetic boots and did this! WE HAVE WITNESSES!
Kirk has no response, is shocked. BONES
Bones is being held back from Gorkon by two Klingons.
He's a DOCTOR!
How can I trust -BONES Are you carrying a surgeon?
(enraged) We were until your torpedoes!
Then let me help!
Chang hesitates; signals. Bones is released.
I need some light. Can we get him

onto the table...? KLINGONS lift Gorkon onto his table; someone produces LIGHT. Bones whips out his medical scanner. His hands are SHAKING. A spage-age REMBRANDT painting. BONES Sweet Jesus...! He's lost a lot of whatever this stuff is...
Can you - ?
Jim, I don't even know his anatomy.
Bones furiously goes over Gorkon with his anabolic photoplaser. Shakes his head.
(continuing) The wounds aren't closing...
You're killing him!
Kirk grabs Chang before he can get to Bones.
No!
Chancellor Gorkon, can you hear me?
No response.
Father...!
Bones...?
He's gone into some kind of arrest... come on, dammit!
He tries a medical tricorder. Frantically, he WHUMPS Gorkon's chest... as Kirk et al, look, Gorkon feebly opens his eyes, sees Kirk...
Are you all right?
HE FREEZES; Bones looks at the scanner. It burns bright red. Gorkon is dead. Kirk is in shock: he was for real. Azetbur sits holding Gorkon, dry-eyed. Kirk has to pull the stunned Bones away.
